  double-restrictions \((f_i)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) and \((f_j)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) reach the
  overlap by two different slice-restriction routes, and the resulting morphisms live in
  types that are propositionally equal but not definitionally equal, so a homogeneous
  equation between them does not even type-check. What \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) asks for,

  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) and \(\mathtt{localSection}\,j\) to the overlap, as sections
  of the hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) over \(U_i \sqcap U_j\), i.e.\ an equation in the single
  type \(\mathcal{H}(U_i \sqcap U_j)\). The bridge from one to the other transports the
  heterogeneous agreement through the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) conjugation built into
  \(\mathtt{localSection}\) (its sectionwise components and naturality,
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_hom_local_section}). Concretely, restricting
  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) to the overlap evaluates sectionwise to the corresponding
  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications,

5673	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i \in \mathcal{H}(U_i)\): its
5674	  component at a pair \((V, h : V \to U_i)\) is the given component of \(f_i\), conjugated
5675	  by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications arising from the open-set equality
5676	  \(\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}(V)) = V\) valid for \(V \subseteq U_i\). The non-trivial content is
5677	  the \emph{naturality condition}: these \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugated components commute
5678	  across the morphisms of the slice category over \(U_i\) --- precisely the
5679	  section-direction slice of \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}, with coherence
5680	  automatic on the thin poset of opens of \(X\) by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). This
5681	  is the standalone sub-lemma the gluing of \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
5682	  is built upon.
5683	\end{lemma}
5684	
5685	\begin{lemma}
5686	
5687	\leanok
5688	[Compatible local morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global morphism]
5689	  \label{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat}
5690	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat}
5691	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and \(\{U_i\}\) an
5692	  open cover of \(X\). Suppose given, for each \(i\), a morphism
5693	  \(f_i \colon M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_i\) such that
5694	  for all \(i, j\) the restrictions of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) to \(U_i \cap U_j\) agree.
5695	  Then there is a unique global morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5696	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) whose restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\).
5697	\end{lemma}
5698	
5699	\begin{proof}
5700	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_homMk, lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}
5701	  A morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is, concretely, a morphism of the
5702	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups that is in addition \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear
5703	  sectionwise. We build \(f\) in two steps.
5704	
5705	  \emph{(i) Glue the underlying ab-sheaf morphism.} Forget \(M\) and \(N\) to their
5706	  underlying sheaves of abelian groups. The morphisms-between-them presheaf
5707	  \(\mathcal{H} := \mathtt{presheafHom}\,(M.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\,
5708	  (N.\mathtt{val}.\mathtt{presheaf})\), whose value at an open \(W\) is the abelian
5709	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
5710	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle
5711	  \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom}\), which consumes the sheaf condition of \(N\).
5712	  Regarding \(\mathcal{H}\) as a \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\), its universal gluing
5713	  property \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) amalgamates any compatible family of local
5714	  sections of \(\mathcal{H}\) into a unique global section. This route is preferred over
5715	  the raw sieve-level amalgamation \(\mathtt{presheafHom\_isSheafFor}\), which carries
5716	  strictly more bookkeeping.
5717	
5718	  The load-bearing datum is, for each \(i\), the \emph{local section}
5719	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i \in \mathcal{H}.\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,U_i)\)
5720	  of \cref{lem:scheme_modules_hom_local_section},
5721	  manufactured from \(f_i\). Its component at a pair \((V, h : V \to U_i)\) is the given
5722	  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications
5723	  that arise from the open-set equality \(\iota_i\bigl(\iota_i^{-1}(V)\bigr) = V\) valid
5724	  for \(V \subseteq U_i\) (the image under the open immersion \(\iota_i : U_i \to X\) of
5725	  the preimage of \(V\) is \(V\) again). The genuine coherence risk of the whole
5726	  construction is concentrated in the \emph{naturality condition} of
5727	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\): the requirement that these
5728	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugated components of \(f_i\) commute across the morphisms of
5729	  the slice category \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\). This is the one sub-piece to be
5730	  built and verified first, as a standalone lemma.
5731	
5732	  This \(\mathtt{localSection}\) naturality is precisely the \emph{section-direction
5733	  slice} of the comparison packaged in \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}:
5734	  converting the local datum \(f_i\), which lives over the open-immersion pullback
5735	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,\widetilde{U_i}\), into a section of \(\mathcal{H}\) indexed over the
5736	  slice \(\mathtt{Over}\,U_i\) is the one-sided realisation of the open-immersion
5737	  \(\leftrightarrow\) slice transport. As there, the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugation is
5738	  controlled by the down-set identity \(\iota_i(\iota_i^{-1}(V)) = V\) for
5739	  \(V \subseteq U_i\), and the coherence is automatic on the thin poset
5740	  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\). This A-engine uses the
5741	  sheaf-site equivalence \cref{lem:open_immersion_slice_sheaf_equiv}; the C-bridge
5742	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, by contrast, uses a per-open slice comparison at
5743	  the presheaf level over the \emph{varying} section ring \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), and so
5744	  does \emph{not} reduce to the same sheaf-site root --- the two are independent and
5745	  must be built separately.
5746	
5747	  Of the remaining four sub-steps, three are mechanical and one --- the cocycle bridge
5748	  (a) --- carries the genuine content. We spell it out.
5749	
5750	  \emph{(a) The cocycle/\(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) condition on the family
5751	  \((\mathtt{localSection}\,i)_i\).} The overlap-agreement hypothesis is phrased with
5752	  \emph{heterogeneous} equality (\(\mathtt{HEq}\)) rather than ordinary equality: the two
5753	  double-restrictions \((f_i)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) and \((f_j)|_{U_i \cap U_j}\) reach the
5754	  overlap by two different slice-restriction routes, and the resulting morphisms live in
5755	  types that are propositionally equal but not definitionally equal, so a homogeneous
5756	  equation between them does not even type-check. What \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) asks for,
5757	  by contrast, is an \emph{honest} equality of the two restrictions of the local sections
5758	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) and \(\mathtt{localSection}\,j\) to the overlap, as sections
5759	  of the hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) over \(U_i \sqcap U_j\), i.e.\ an equation in the single
5760	  type \(\mathcal{H}(U_i \sqcap U_j)\). The bridge from one to the other transports the
5761	  heterogeneous agreement through the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) conjugation built into
5762	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\) (its sectionwise components and naturality,
5763	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_hom_local_section}). Concretely, restricting
5764	  \(\mathtt{localSection}\,i\) to the overlap evaluates sectionwise to the corresponding
5765	  component of \(f_i\), conjugated by the canonical \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) identifications,
5766	  and likewise for \(j\); once both sides are transported into the common type
5767	  \(\mathcal{H}(U_i \sqcap U_j)\) along those \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)s, the route-difference
5768	  between the two restriction maps collapses --- any two parallel morphisms in the thin
5769	  poset \((\mathtt{Opens}\,X)^{\mathrm{op}}\) are equal, by \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\)
5770	  --- and the heterogeneous agreement becomes the required genuine equality of the two
5771	  transported sections. Thus the \(\mathtt{IsCompatible}\) condition is exactly the assumed
5772	  agreement of \(f_i\) and \(f_j\) on the overlaps \(U_i \cap U_j\), recovered after the
5773	  \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-transport and the \(\mathtt{Subsingleton.elim}\) identification of
5774	  the comparison maps.
5775	
5776	  The other three sub-steps are mechanical: (b) gluing via
5777	  \(\mathtt{existsUnique\_gluing}\) and converting the amalgamated section over
5778	  \(\mathrm{op}\,\top\) into a genuine morphism \(g\) of the underlying presheaves of
5779	  \(M\) and \(N\) --- the
5780	  \(\top\)-section-to-morphism passage being the global-sections equivalence
5781	  \(\mathtt{presheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) / \(\mathtt{sheafHomSectionsEquiv}\) of the
5782	  hom-sheaf \(\mathcal{H}\) (which, since \(\top\) is terminal in \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\),
5783	  lands the glued section as an honest morphism of presheaves), \emph{not} a hand-unfold
5784	  of the \(\top\)-section; (c) the
5785	  sectionwise linearity check; and (d) the recovery that the restriction of \(g\) to
5786	  each \(U_i\) is the ab-sheaf morphism underlying \(f_i\).
5787	
5788	  \emph{(ii) Promote to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear.} The linearity equation
5789	  \(g(r \cdot m) = r \cdot g(m)\) is a sectionwise identity between two sections of a
5790	  separated presheaf. It holds on each \(U_i\), because there \(g\) restricts to the
5791	  module morphism \(f_i\); since the two sides agree on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) and the

5793	  global linearity through the scheme-level wrapper \cref{def:scheme_modules_homMk}
5794	  (\(\mathtt{homMk}\)) yields a morphism \(f \colon M \to N\) in
5795	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); its restriction to each \(U_i\) is \(f_i\) and its
5796	  uniqueness are both inherited from the ab-sheaf amalgamation of step (i).
